description.task: Implement bottom-up Fibonacci using a DP table. Fill the table iteratively from f(0) up to f(n). Also implement a space-optimized version that only keeps the last two values instead of the full table.
instructions: Table approach uses O(n) space; optimized uses O(1) space — same O(n) time
description.expectedOutput:
fib( 0) =        0, optimized =        0, match = true
fib( 1) =        1, optimized =        1, match = true
fib( 5) =        5, optimized =        5, match = true
fib(10) =       55, optimized =       55, match = true
fib(20) =     6765, optimized =     6765, match = true
fib(30) =   832040, optimized =   832040, match = true

description.task: Implement LCS (Longest Common Subsequence) using bottom-up tabulation. Build a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] = LCS length of a[0..i-1] and b[0..j-1]. Also reconstruct the actual LCS string by backtracking through the table.
instructions: AGGTAB and GXTXAYB: LCS length=4, one LCS='GTAB'
ABCBDAB and BDCABA: LCS length=4, one LCS string backtrack produces 'BCBA' or 'BDAB'
The backtrack logic is already provided — you only need to fill the table
description.expectedOutput:
LCS length: 4
LCS string: GTAB
LCS length: 4
LCS string: BCBA

description.task: Implement 0/1 Knapsack using bottom-up tabulation. Build a 2D dp table where dp[i][w] = max value using first i items with capacity w. Also determine which items were selected by backtracking the table.
instructions: cap=5: take item 3(w=5,v=7) → value 7; selected item indices=[3]
The selectedItems method backtracks the dp table — it's already provided
Only the dp[i][w] update in maxValue needs to be completed
description.expectedOutput:
Max value (cap=7): 9
Items selected:    [1, 2]
Max value (cap=5): 7
Items selected:    [3]
